text.title: structured-output-mode-picker Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for structured-output-mode-picker as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using response_format={"type": "json_object"} for extraction that downstream code expects to be schema-conformant.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: json_object guarantees only that json.loads() succeeds — field names, types, enums, required-ness are all free. Pydantic parse fails on roughly every malformed batch and the failure is intermittent, not signal-rich.

failure-mode.description: Modeling agent actions as a discriminated union under SO and dispatching on the discriminator field in user code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tool calls already give you the dispatch affordance natively (function name + typed args + provider-side validation + multi-tool selection). Doing it via SO discriminator forces a custom router that re-implements what the tool API already does and forfeits parallel tool calls.

failure-mode.description: Forcing SQL, regex, GBNF, or other non-JSON output through a tool call argument as a single string field.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The model still has to generate the DSL inside an unconstrained string — no grammar mask applies inside string values. Use a real grammar (Outlines/XGrammar/GBNF) on a model that supports it; XGrammar reports 100x throughput vs string-inside-JSON workarounds.
